category_title: At-risk students in the Normal Technical stream exhibiting attention-seeking or disengaged behaviors
minimal_context: The student is in the keyboard lab using SONAR software to learn their instrumental part for 'Count On Me' by listening to pre-recorded tracks.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please put on your headphones and use the SONAR software to listen to the pre-recorded tracks. Your task is to discover and learn your instrumental part for 'Count On Me'.

STUDENT: (Looks at the computer screen and the keyboard, appearing a bit blurred about the task.)

TUTOR: Keep trying to figure it out. Listen to the recording.

STUDENT: (Does not say anything at all, making it hard to know whether he knows or does not know what to do.)

TUTOR: The others are already focused on learning their parts. You need to start practicing.

STUDENT: (Remains completely silent and stops doing his work for some time, visibly frustrated because he cannot figure out what to do.)

options.A: Terminate demands and remove the headphones and software for 10 seconds contingent upon the challenging behavior.
options.B: Walk around to make sure the student is not only on task but actually understands what is going on.
options.C: Ignore all undesirable behaviors exhibited by the student during the activity in the keyboard lab.
options.D: Allow the student to withdraw from the keyboard lab for a time-limited period to prevent emotional overload.
